messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Are you putting yours up at home @Cupra0 ? I can see your client node on the map, I presume you're not reaching anyone with it? I've got two clients here, one will be flashed as a repeater and going over my roof on as long a pole as I can get a 10m USB cable up, as soon as I have at least one other client within range. I'm not detecting anyone within range from the attic with a 4.5 dBi Paradar antenna on a Heltec V4 - not that I would expect to from looking at the map...

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Newmilns, so stuck down in a valley like you. Are you going to try to link into Mr Ben’s G13 Repeater in Jordanhill? Looking at the antenna coverage map (if it can be believed) it seems that you only just miss reaching them, but I imagine both of your locations are approximate & I don’t know your heights. It suggests that if yours was 25m above ground and theirs 10m you might just make contact. Corse Hill in the Whitelee wind park seems to gets better line of site into Dumbarton and Glasgow than most, (even better than one on at 45m on the Erskin bridge, or most mountains I’ve tried to the North) the map suggests it could link you (assuming you’re between Glasgow Road and Stirling Road) with Jordanhill. A single well placed repeater could then link you with me also.

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Yea, it’s confusing, there are two separate maps. 1) To get your repeater showing on the public map I believe you need to manually add it. 2) The Antenna Coverage map is under Tools in the app, but I think all may not be as it appears: Liam suggested it shows full line of sight rather than actual TX reach. I’m investigating.
messages.content_html: Yea, it’s confusing, there are two separate maps. 1) To get your repeater showing on the public map I believe you need to manually add it. 2) The Antenna Coverage map is under Tools in the app, but I think all may not be as it appears: Liam suggested it shows full line of sight rather than actual TX reach. I’m investigating.

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: For the one on the roof the plan is to just use a 10m USB and a backup battery up the pole. I don’t plan to put them up until the new year though. Need to plan the backbone here and determine optimal locations. I’ll likely put my repeater up at the same time as its nearest neighbour.

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Yea, antenna coverage currently only shows you line of sight, not actual reach. I’m trying to figure out the best way to make a fix available to @Liam Cottle who wrote the app. It’s closed source which means I first need to get a version of the antenna coverage feature working (done) and demonstrate different options (kind of done, works for one option at a time, but something inexplicable is happening when I try to overlay them). (edited)
messages.content_html: Yea, antenna coverage currently only shows you line of sight, not actual reach. I’m trying to figure out the best way to make a fix available to @Liam Cottle who wrote the app. It’s closed source which means I first need to get a version of the antenna coverage feature working (done) and demonstrate different options (kind of done, works for one option at a time, but something inexplicable is happening when I try to overlay them). (edited)

messages.content_html: If you go in tools you can see coverage

messages.author: Aqueum
messages.text: Exactly the tool I'm talking about, and exactly the problem with the current system. Users assume that because there is a Line of Sight tool, the Antenna Coverage tool isn't just Line of Sight but is actual Antenna Coverage. @Liam Cottle if you're reading this - please let me know how you'd like me to share the easiest update for you, so that people don't get disappointed when they can't reach red-zones in the Antenna Coverage tool.
messages.content_html: Exactly the tool I'm talking about, and exactly the problem with the current system. Users assume that because there is a Line of Sight tool, the Antenna Coverage tool isn't just Line of Sight but is actual Antenna Coverage. @Liam Cottle if you're reading this - please let me know how you'd like me to share the easiest update for you, so that people don't get disappointed when they can't reach red-zones in the Antenna Coverage tool.

messages.author: Stevie_D (ROG)
messages.text: I was completing some routine maintenance on one of our MT routers. I am not advocating one platform or the other, but you guys should be aware the existing MT mesh spans from Kilcreggan in the West, to Anstruther in the East and North to Carnoustie. You dont need to be isloated while waiting for the MC mesh to build (i dont doubt it will eventually build, but its lacking willing volunteers just now). I am happy to share learnings and advice guys. In fact, the best advice i can give just now is for you to use the website "heywhatsthat.com" to plan your repeater deployments. this gives very accurate line of site detials and can factor in the 868Mhz propagation, so you can tell if the signal will bend over any hills or obstructions (edited)
messages.content_html: I was completing some routine maintenance on one of our MT routers. I am not advocating one platform or the other, but you guys should be aware the existing MT mesh spans from Kilcreggan in the West, to Anstruther in the East and North to Carnoustie. You dont need to be isloated while waiting for the MC mesh to build (i dont doubt it will eventually build, but its lacking willing volunteers just now). I am happy to share learnings and advice guys. In fact, the best advice i can give just now is for you to use the website "heywhatsthat.com" to plan your repeater deployments. this gives very accurate line of site detials and can factor in the 868Mhz propagation, so you can tell if the signal will bend over any hills or obstructions (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: the meshcore dev team have moved to a new discord server. details and reasons are here: https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/23/the-split Please create a new thread for your region in the https://discord.com/channels/1495203904898728149/1495412453150818585 for your region on the new meshcore.io discord server. https://meshcore.gg

messages.author: Stevie_D (ROG)
messages.text: Created a Scotland channel on the new Discord. We can add regional channels in the future but we are less than 50 active users just now so let’s get all parts of Scotland connected first. A collective goal where we can identify isolated pockets of users and plan repeater deployment to link us all up.
messages.content_html: Created a Scotland channel on the new Discord.
